evaluation_summary.actors: All actors listed in the JSON output are specific to a generic web application and are not traceable to the Berachain documentation. Consequently, all extracted actors are considered hallucinated. Key actors from the Berachain ecosystem (e.g., Validators, $BGT Holders, Protocols, Guardians) are missing.
evaluation_summary.assets: All assets listed are typical for a generic system (e.g., User Data, Payment Information, Session Tokens) and are not found in or supported by the Berachain documentation. All extracted assets are considered hallucinated. Core Berachain assets (e.g., $BERA, $BGT, $HONEY, NFTs, Wallet Private Keys) are missing.
evaluation_summary.components: All components described (e.g., Web UI, API Gateway, specific microservices, traditional databases) are from a generic web application architecture and are not present in or supported by the Berachain documentation. All extracted components are considered hallucinated. Fundamental Berachain components (e.g., Berachain L1, BeaconKit, BEX, Reward Vaults, Wallets) are missing.
evaluation_summary.data_flows: All data flows depict interactions within a generic web application and do not reflect the processes described in the Berachain documentation. All extracted data flows, along with their referenced actors and components, are considered hallucinated. Key Berachain interactions (e.g., staking $BERA, proposing blocks, providing liquidity to BEX, $BGT delegation, governance voting) are missing.
evaluation_summary.trust_boundaries: All trust boundaries are defined for a generic web application architecture and are not supported by the Berachain documentation. The components referenced in `between_ids` are themselves untraceable. All extracted trust boundaries are considered hallucinated. Trust boundaries relevant to the Berachain ecosystem (e.g., Wallet to dApp, dApp to Blockchain) are missing.
evaluation_summary.behaviors: All behaviors describe actions within a generic system (e.g., user submits data via Web UI, admin manages configurations) and are not found in or supported by the Berachain documentation. The initiators and targets of these behaviors are also untraceable. All extracted behaviors are considered hallucinated. Core Berachain behaviors (e.g., securing the network via PoL, participating in on-chain governance, earning $BGT by staking LP tokens) are missing.

findings.description: Actor 'User' (description: 'Users interact with the Web UI to submit and view data.') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation.
Actor 'Admin' (description: 'Admin interacts with Admin Panel to manage system configurations.') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation.
Actor 'Third-party Payment Gateway' (description: 'External system for processing payments...') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation.
Actor 'Auditor' (description: 'Auditors have read-only access to Audit Log Database.') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation.
Actor 'Validator' (responsible for block production, staking $BERA, directing $BGT emissions) is clearly described in the Berachain documentation (e.g., doc_7, doc_20, doc_24) but was not extracted.

Actor 'User / $BGT Holder / Liquidity Provider' (interacts with dApps, provides liquidity, stakes LP tokens, delegates $BGT, participates in governance) is a central figure in the Berachain documentation (e.g., doc_7, doc_10, doc_24, doc_27) but was not extracted with these specific roles and capabilities.
Actor 'Protocol / Ecosystem Project' (deploys Reward Vaults, offers incentives) is described in the Berachain documentation (e.g., doc_14, doc_21, doc_24) but was not extracted.
Actor 'Guardian' (can cancel malicious proposals during timelock) is described in the Berachain documentation (doc_3) but was not extracted.
Asset 'User Data' (description: 'User profile information...') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation.
Asset 'Payment Information' (description: 'Data related to payment processing...') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation.
Asset 'System Configurations' (description: 'Configuration settings for the system...') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation.
Asset 'Audit Logs' (description: 'Logs of significant actions and events...') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation in this form. Berachain has on-chain transaction history.

Asset 'Session Tokens' (description: 'Tokens issued by the Auth Service...') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation. Authentication is typically via cryptographic wallet signatures.

Asset 'API Keys' (description: 'Keys used for securing Internal Service-to-Service Communication.') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation in this context. While RPCs might have authentication, this generic asset is not detailed.

Asset 'Transaction Records' (description: 'Records of financial transactions stored in the Payment Database.') is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation in this form. Berachain has on-chain transactions, not records in a 'Payment Database'.
Asset '$BERA' (native gas and staking token) is a core asset in the Berachain documentation (e.g., doc_26, doc_29) but was not extracted.
Asset '$BGT' (governance and rewards token) is a core asset in the Berachain documentation (e.g., doc_27, doc_29) but was not extracted.
Asset '$HONEY' (native stablecoin) is a core asset in the Berachain documentation (e.g., doc_28) but was not extracted.
Asset 'Berachain NFTs' (e.g., Bong Bears, Bit Bears) are described in the Berachain documentation (doc_4) but were not extracted.

Asset 'LP Tokens / Receipt Tokens' (representing liquidity positions or staked assets) are crucial for PoL and described in the Berachain documentation (e.g., doc_7, doc_18, doc_25) but were not extracted.
Asset 'Wallet Private Keys / Seed Phrase' (used to control user assets on-chain) are fundamental and mentioned in the Berachain documentation (doc_5) but were not extracted.
Component 'Web UI' is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation as a core chain component. Users interact via dApps like BeraHub.
Component 'Admin Panel' is not explicitly supported by the Berachain documentation. Chain governance is via $BGT voting.

Component 'API Gateway' as a centralized entry point for all external requests is not how interaction with a decentralized blockchain like Berachain is primarily described. Users interact with RPC nodes or dApp frontends.
Component 'Auth Service' managing session tokens is not explicitly supported. Authentication is typically via wallet signatures.
Component 'User Service' managing user profiles in a 'User Database' is not explicitly supported. User data is generally on-chain or managed by dApps in a decentralized manner.
Component 'Payment Service' integrating with a 'Third-party Payment Gateway' and storing records in a 'Payment Database' is not explicitly supported. Payments are on-chain transactions.
Component 'Config Service' managing system configurations in a 'Config Database' is not explicitly supported. Chain parameters are managed by governance.
Component 'User Database' is not explicitly supported as a core Berachain component.
Component 'Payment Database' is not explicitly supported as a core Berachain component.
Component 'Config Database' is not explicitly supported as a core Berachain component.
Component 'Audit Log Service' and 'Internal Message Queue' for off-chain audit logging are not explicitly supported. Auditing is primarily via on-chain history.
Component 'Audit Log Database' is not explicitly supported as a core Berachain component.
Component 'Internal Message Queue' is not explicitly supported as a core Berachain component in this generic form.
Component 'Berachain L1 Blockchain' (the core network itself) is fundamental in the Berachain documentation (e.g., doc_9) but was not extracted.
Component 'BeaconKit' (modular consensus layer) is a key technology in the Berachain documentation (doc_8, doc_9) but was not extracted.
Component 'EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine)' (execution environment) is central to Berachain's functionality (doc_9) but was not extracted.
Component 'BEX (Berachain Exchange)' (native DEX) is a core dApp in the Berachain documentation (doc_11, doc_13) but was not extracted.
Component 'Reward Vaults' (smart contracts for staking and earning $BGT) are crucial to PoL and described in the Berachain documentation (doc_3, doc_7, doc_25) but were not extracted.
Component 'BeraHub' (central dApp for managing $BGT, accessing BEX, governance) is described in the Berachain documentation (doc_13) but was not extracted.
Component 'Wallets' (e.g., MetaMask, for users to interact with the chain) are mentioned in the Berachain documentation (doc_5) but were not extracted.
Component 'Berachain Bridge' (for transferring assets to/from Berachain) is described in the Berachain documentation (doc_6) but was not extracted.
Component 'Governance Contracts' (for on-chain decision making) are described in the Berachain documentation (doc_3) but were not extracted.

Data flow 'Validator stakes $BERA to participate in consensus' is a core process in Berachain (doc_7, doc_20, doc_26) but was not extracted.
Data flow 'User provides liquidity to a BEX pool and receives LP tokens' is a key interaction for PoL (doc_7, doc_11, doc_25) but was not extracted.
Data flow 'User stakes LP tokens in a Reward Vault to earn $BGT' is fundamental to Berachain's PoL (doc_7, doc_18, doc_25) but was not extracted.
Data flow 'Validator directs $BGT emissions to Reward Vaults and receives Incentives' is a core PoL mechanism (doc_7, doc_21) but was not extracted.
Data flow 'User ($BGT Holder) delegates $BGT to a Validator to boost rewards and earn a share of Incentives' is a key PoL interaction (doc_7, doc_16, doc_21, doc_27) but was not extracted.
Data flow 'User creates/votes on a governance proposal using $BGT via BeraHub/Governance Contracts' is a key Berachain process (doc_3, doc_13) but was not extracted.

Trust boundary 'Berachain Blockchain (Consensus Layer/BeaconKit) <-> Validators' is essential for network security but was not extracted.
Trust boundary 'Berachain <-> External Blockchains (e.g., Ethereum via Berachain Bridge)' is important for asset movement but was not extracted.

Behavior 'Validator Secures Network and Earns Rewards' (by staking $BERA, proposing blocks, earning $BGT and fees) is a core Berachain behavior (doc_7, doc_10, doc_20) but was not extracted.

Behavior 'User Provides Liquidity and Earns $BGT' (by depositing assets in BEX, staking LP tokens in Reward Vaults) is a fundamental PoL behavior (doc_7, doc_11, doc_25, doc_27) but was not extracted.
Behavior 'User Participates in Governance' (by delegating $BGT or voting on proposals) is a key Berachain behavior (doc_3, doc_13, doc_27) but was not extracted.
Behavior 'User Swaps Tokens on BEX' is a primary user activity on Berachain (doc_11, doc_13) but was not extracted.
Behavior 'User Bridges Assets to/from Berachain' is an important ecosystem interaction (doc_4, doc_6) but was not extracted.
